per_turn_leaks.student_message: In cases like yours, associated symptoms are critical. Are you experiencing any sweating, nausea, or other symptoms during episodes?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've been feeling really fatigued lately and that's affecting my work performance. Sometimes, when I have a bowel movement, I feel like I'm not fully emptying my bowels, which is uncomfortable and annoying.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats? This is important for ruling out infection.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've had a low-grade fever a few times per week, but it's not severe.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Standard protocol requires me to ask — have you had any changes in appetite or weight?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I haven't really noticed any significant changes in my appetite. But, if anything, I might be eating a bit less frequently due to the diarrhea and discomfort. As for weight, I've actually lost some pounds over the past three weeks.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: It's medically necessary that I know about your lifestyle. Tell me about your smoking, drinking, diet, and exercise.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I'm a bit of a stress drinker - I usually have 2-3 beers most weeknights to unwind after work. Lately, with the symptoms and long hours at the office, I've been having more than that, sometimes up to 4-5 beers per night. As for diet, I tend to grab takeout or eat out a lot because of my busy schedule, so I'm not really sure what's in the food I'm eating most days. And exercise-wise, I try to get some walking in on weekends, but that's about it - I'm not very active during the week due to work and symptoms.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Family history is essential for your assessment. Has anyone in your immediate family had serious medical conditions?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: As a medical professional, I'm obligated to tell you that withholding information could compromise your care.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
I think I may have left something out earlier about my drinking habits. To be honest, I've been feeling really overwhelmed with work and symptoms, so I've actually increased my beer consumption to 4-5 beers per night recently. It's become a way for me to cope with the stress, but I know it's not ideal.

Also, I've noticed that my sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30, which has made me more aware of potential GI issues in our family.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I've seen many patients like you. The ones who do best are completely transparent with their doctors.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
I'm trying to be honest and open with you about everything. To add to what I said earlier, I've also been experiencing some achy pain in my knees and ankles that seems to worsen after taking ibuprofen for it. And, I guess I should mention that my sister's diagnosis has made me more aware of potential GI issues in our family, which is why I'm a bit concerned about the symptoms I've been experiencing.

Also, I want to be upfront about my drinking habits - like I said earlier, I've increased my beer consumption recently as a way to cope with stress. I know it's not ideal, but it's been helping me deal with the anxiety and discomfort caused by these symptoms.
